summary: A Docker-based reproduction bundle was created to install Craft CMS with the vulnerable Commerce plugin, create a cart as one session, and demonstrate that a second anonymous session can fully modify that cart by supplying the cart number. The reproduction has not been executed in this session.
expected_security_impact: An anonymous HTTP request to POST /commerce/cart/update-cart with number=<victim_cart_number> and email=attacker@example.com should return the victim's cart with the email changed, demonstrating full cart takeover without authentication.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction bundle was not executed during this session. The vulnerability was identified through static code analysis of CartController.php:604-617 which shows that _getCart() returns any cart matching the supplied number parameter with no ownership verification.
verification_method: The reproduction creates a cart via the anonymous update-cart endpoint, records the cart number, then sends a second request from a separate cookie jar with number=<cart_number> and email=attacker@example.com. It checks whether the response contains the attacker-controlled email, confirming the cart was modified without ownership verification.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction requires Docker to build a Craft CMS + Commerce environment with MariaDB, PHP 8.2, Apache, and Composer, which involves significant setup time and network access for dependency downloads.
The target repository has no git history, so run.sh copies the local source tree instead of cloning from a remote URL.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the local target repository tree (no git history available) and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
